faqs.question: How can I verify BeiLuo is an authorized Genuway distributor?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo Electronics is a core distributor of Genuway Technology products. You can verify our authorization by contacting Genuway directly or requesting our distributor certificate. We provide 100% genuine products with full manufacturer warranty.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for distributor verification.

faqs.question: What technical documentation is available for Genuway products?
faqs.answer: Complete documentation includes datasheets, application notes, reliability reports, and qualification certificates. All documentation is available in English. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical team for specific documentation requests.

articles.title: How to Select the Right Crystal Oscillator

author.name: Michael Chen
author.title: Senior FAE - Timing Products
author.bio: Michael Chen is a Senior Field Application Engineer specializing in timing products and crystal oscillator applications.

articles.summary: Comprehensive guide to selecting crystal oscillators based on stability, frequency, and application requirements.
content: This technical reference document provides detailed information about genuway product specifications, characteristics, and performance parameters. Use this information to support your design and analysis activities.

Electrical characteristics are specified over the operating temperature range unless otherwise noted. Parameters are guaranteed by design, testing, or statistical analysis. Typical values represent the most likely parametric norm at 25°C.

Thermal characteristics require careful attention during system design. The junction-to-ambient thermal resistance depends on the mounting configuration, PCB copper area, and airflow conditions. Use thermal simulation tools to predict operating temperatures under actual conditions.

Reliability data is based on accelerated life testing and field failure analysis. Mean time between failures (MTBF) calculations follow industry-standard methodologies. Contact BeiLuo for detailed reliability reports and qualification data.

faeInsights.insight: Most designers underestimate the importance of temperature stability.
faeInsights.logic: Select oscillator type based on stability requirements and budget.
bestPractices: Verify phase noise for RF apps
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this How to Select the Right Crystal Oscillator article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

customerCases.problem: Customer had timing issues in base station design.
customerCases.diagnosis: Inadequate oscillator stability for the application.
customerCases.solution: Upgraded to TCXO with better stability.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SPXO and TCXO?
faqs.answer: SPXO is a simple packaged crystal oscillator without temperature compensation. TCXO includes temperature compensation circuitry for improved stability. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SPXO for cost-sensitive apps, TCXO for better stability.

articles.title: PCB Layout Guidelines for Crystal Oscillators
articles.summary: Comprehensive PCB layout guidelines for crystal oscillators to ensure optimal performance and minimize EMI.
content: Proper PCB layout is critical for crystal oscillator performance. This guide covers best practices for placement, routing, and grounding.
Place the crystal close to the IC, typically within 10mm. Keep traces short and symmetrical for differential oscillators.
Use a solid ground plane under the crystal area. Avoid high-speed signals near crystal traces to prevent coupling.
For load capacitors, place them close to the crystal pins. Use matching capacitors for better frequency accuracy.

faqs.question: How close should the crystal be to the MCU?
faqs.answer: Keep the crystal within 10-20mm of the MCU. Shorter traces reduce parasitic capacitance and improve stability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Place crystal as close as possible to MCU within layout constraints.
